seo.title: AI Practice Intelligence Agent for Law Firms | Dodonai
seo.description: Roll up matter health, partner workload, settlement velocity, and revenue per practice area into a Monday picture you can act on. The dashboard you keep meaning to build.

hero.headline: AI Practice Intelligence Agent
hero.subheadline: Pulls from your practice management system, accounting, calendar, and email to produce a weekly partners' digest. Matter health, workload distribution, settlement velocity, and revenue trend, in one place, every Monday at 8am.
bullets: Weekly Monday digest delivered before the partners' meeting
Matter-health scoring with red, yellow, green per active file
Workload distribution across partners and practice areas
Revenue and collection trends by practice area and matter type
hero.guarantee: Blueprint refunded if we don't leave you with a clear path forward.

scenario.heading: Monday morning, before the partners' meeting
paragraphs: It's 7:55 on a Monday. The 4 partners of a 14-attorney firm gather in the conference room with coffee. The Practice Intelligence agent has already posted the weekly digest in their shared Slack channel: 132 active matters, 7 flagged red (movement stalled, deadline approaching, or client unresponsive), 18 yellow, the rest green. Workload chart shows one partner carrying 38% of the active personal injury inventory while another is at 14% and asking for more. Revenue line for family law is up 9% on the trailing 90 days; for real estate, down 22%.

The meeting that used to start with each partner reading from their own notes now starts with a shared picture. The conversation moves to action: redistribute 6 personal injury matters, pause the real estate marketing spend, intervene on 3 of the 7 red matters this week. Decisions made by 8:30 instead of by 10.

whatItDoes.heading: What the Practice Intelligence agent does
paragraphs: The agent pulls structured and unstructured data from across your firm's systems weekly. From the practice management system: matter status, age, last activity, partner ownership. From accounting: billable hours, realization rate, collection percentage, AR aging. From the calendar and email: how often each matter has been worked, whether the client has been contacted, what's coming up. It rolls those signals into a partners' digest that takes 5 minutes to read instead of an hour to produce.

The matter-health score is the synthesis. Red flags surface matters where multiple signals point the same direction (no activity in 30 days, deadline inside 14 days, client silence over 21 days). The point is not to grade attorneys; the point is to make sure no matter falls through the cracks because everyone assumed someone else was watching.

inputsOutputs.heading: Inputs and outputs
inputs: Practice management system matter and activity records
Accounting system data (QuickBooks, Xero, LeanLaw, TrustBooks)
Calendar feed across all partners and associates
Time-entry data for billable hour and realization analysis
outputs: Monday partners' digest delivered by 8am to a shared Slack channel
Matter-health roll-up with red/yellow/green and reasons cited
Workload distribution by partner, associate, and practice area
Revenue and collection trends with quarter-over-quarter comparison
Pipeline view by stage (intake, active, settlement, collection, closed)
Quarterly deep-dive report for partner-level strategic review

integrations: Clio, MyCase, Practice Panther, SmokeBall, or NetDocuments
QuickBooks Online, Xero, LeanLaw, or TrustBooks
Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 for calendar and email signals
Slack or Microsoft Teams for digest delivery
Read-only access; no system the agent reads from is modified

safety.heading: Safety posture
paragraphs: The agent is read-only across every system it touches. It produces analysis and routes it to the partners. It doesn't change matter status, doesn't reassign work, doesn't adjust billing entries. The interventions that follow the digest are made by humans with the right authority.

Sensitive practice data (revenue, partner compensation signals, individual attorney performance) is scoped to the audience that should see it. Partner-only views go to partners. Practice-area views can be shared more broadly if your firm prefers transparency. The access controls are explicit and tunable during Build.

faq.question: How is this different from the dashboards in our practice management system?
faq.answer: Native practice management system dashboards show what's in the practice management system. They don't synthesize across accounting, calendar, and email to surface the matter-health pattern. The Practice Intelligence agent pulls from all of those, weighs the signals, and produces a recommendation per matter rather than a list of metrics to interpret.

faq.question: Will the matter-health scoring create friction with attorneys?
faq.answer: It can if it's framed as evaluation. We frame it as visibility. The score is about the matter, not the attorney; a red flag means the matter needs attention, not that someone failed. Firms that have rolled this out successfully introduce it as a tool the partners use to support the team, with the team seeing the same picture the partners see.

faq.question: Does it account for matter complexity in the workload distribution?
faq.answer: Yes. Workload isn't just matter count; it's weighted by matter type, hours-to-date, and stage. A litigator carrying 8 active depositions is treated very differently from one carrying 8 dormant probate files. The weighting is calibrated during Build with input from the partners.

faq.question: What if our accounting system is older or our practice management system is custom?
faq.answer: We've integrated with the major systems and several less-common ones. For older systems with no API, we use scheduled exports or read-only database connections. For a truly custom practice management system, the Build phase includes the integration work. The Blueprint phase identifies the integration cost up front.

faq.question: How do we keep the digest from becoming wallpaper that nobody reads?
faq.answer: Two things keep it readable. First, the digest is short by default (1 page, scannable in 5 minutes), and only expands when something requires attention. Second, the partners' meeting is structured around the digest, so reading it has a purpose. The Managed retainer reviews engagement quarterly and tunes the format if attention drops.
